title: Topic Marker は
description: The particle 'wa' introduces the topic of your sentence. Think of it as 'As for...', or 'Speaking of...'.
description_tm: 'Wa' bölejigi sözlemiň temasyny kesgitleýär. Ony 'Şu barada aýtsak...' diýen ýaly düşünip bilersiňiz.

title: The Verb です
description: 'Desu' is the polite way to say 'to be' (am, is, are). It always comes at the end of the sentence.
description_tm: 'Desu' - 'bolmak' diýen manyny berýän sylaşykly sözdür. Ol elmydama sözlemiň ahyrynda gelýär.

title: Question Particle か
description: Adding 'ka' to the end of a sentence turns it into a question. It acts like a spoken question mark.
description_tm: Sözlemiň ahyryna 'ka' goşmak ony sorag sözlemine öwürýär. Ol sorag belgisi ýaly hyzmat edýär.

examples.jp: 日本語は難しいですか。

examples_tm.jp: 日本語は難しいですか。

title: Possessive Particle の
description: 'No' connects two nouns. It usually shows ownership, similar to 's in English.
description_tm: 'No' iki ady birleşdirýär. Ol adatça eýeçiligi ýa-da degişliligi aňladýär.

title: Object Marker を
description: The particle 'wo' (pronounced 'o') marks the direct object—the thing that is being acted upon.
description_tm: 'Wo' bölejigi ( 'o' diýlip okalýar) gönümel obýekti, ýagny işiň haýsy zada gönükdirilendigini aňladýar.

title: Direction に
description: 'Ni' marks the destination or the point in time. It's used with verbs like 'go', 'come', or 'return'.
description_tm: 'Ni' barmaly ýeriňizi ýa-da anyk bir wagty aňlatmak üçin ulanylýar. Ol 'gitmek', 'gelmek' ýa-da 'gaýdyp gelmek' ýaly işlikler bilen ulanylýar.

title: Location Particle で
description: 'De' marks the location where an action takes place (where you DO something).
description_tm: 'De' bölejigi işiň ýerine ýetirilýän ýerini (bir zady EDÝÄN ýeriňizi) aňlatmak üçin ulanylýar.

title: Existence あります
title_tm: Barlyk(jansyz zatlar üçin) あります
description: Used to say that an inanimate object (a thing) exists or that you have it.
description_tm: Jansyz zadyň bardygyny ýa-da sende şol zadyň bardygyny aýtmak üçin ulanylýar.

title: Existence います
description: Used for the existence of living things like people and animals.
description_tm: Adamlar we haýwanlar ýaly janly zatlaryň bardygyny aýtmak üçin ulanylýar.

title: Adjectives (い vs な)
description: Japanese has two types of adjectives. 'I' adjectives end in 'i', while 'Na' adjectives need 'na' before a noun.
description_tm: Ýapon dilinde sypatlaryň iki görnüşi bar. 'I' sypatlary 'i' harpy bilen tamamlanýar, emma 'Na' sypatlary atdan öň 'na' goşulmasyny talap edýär.
